title: How to properly write his name
display_title: How to properly write his name
wikitext:
[]: How to properly write his name?             Quote Post by Sventevith » Mon Aug 01, 2005 9:04 pm Is it Gendou, Gendo, Gendoh or something whole different? I've read so damn many, I don't really know... and I know 0% about kanji and so - I can't check it ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 01, 2005 9:10 pm Gendo's name is written in katakana -- kanji is something completely different. (Google will tell you the difference.) All three spellings are valid. When Gendo's name is written in Roman letters in Japanese merch, it's commonly "Gendo". However, the "o" IS actually long; the spellings "Gendou" and "Gendoh" reflect this. Read this if you want a more thorough explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Mon Aug 01, 2005 9:13 pm Reichu wrote: Gendo's name is written in katakana -- kanji is something completely different. (Google will tell you the difference.) All three spellings are valid. When Gendo's name is written in Roman letters in Japanese merch, it's commonly "Gendo". However, the "o" IS actually long; the spellings "Gendou" and "Gendoh" reflect this. Read this if you want a more thorough explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Aug 02, 2005 12:43 am Other characters names that have more than one common Romanization. Kaworu / Kaoru Ryohji / Ryoji Touji / Toji In the case of the names Gendo, Touji, and Ryohji, there is a long o sound. ge n do u to u ji ryo u ji In the case of Kaworu, there is a wo which gets slurred into an o ka wo ru

[]:             Quote Post by Zooma » Tue Aug 02, 2005 3:41 am Yep, as everyone has said the "u" in romanization equals a long sound in Japanese, for anything after o that is.. And yeah, wo is pronounced o

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Wed Aug 03, 2005 1:42 pm I say "Kaworu", "Touji" and "Gendou" or "Gendoh"... Never written "Ryohji" before, but I guess I would spell it like that...

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Wed Aug 10, 2005 12:46 pm don't forget souryuu/sohryuu/soryuu/soryu/souryu/sohryu. sorry. i'm insane like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Wed Aug 10, 2005 1:27 pm don't forget souryuu/sohryuu/soryuu/soryu/souryu/sohryu.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Wed Aug 10, 2005 1:35 pm toiletduck wrote: don't forget souryuu/sohryuu/soryuu/soryu/souryu/sohryu. sorry. i'm insane like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Thu Aug 11, 2005 7:44 am Sventevith wrote: Yeah... damn these Japanese names! :P I will just learn how to write them in Japanese...

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Aug 11, 2005 11:23 am I always wondered about the Rei meaning. I don't know which Kanji is usually used for naming in Japan, as Rei is a true name. Yes, it can mean zero, but there are other interesting meanings: 伶 - Actor 冷 - cold, cool 儷 - companion 礼 - expression of gratitude 霊 - soul; spirit; departed soul; ghost 令 - command; order; dictation
